refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) for operational use, ensuring data accuracy and availability for all relevant stakeholders.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is decomposed to explicitly address timeliness, data accuracy, and availability, which are critical for operational effectiveness and safety in a high-risk environment.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the 95th percentile latency of worksite model updates under representative operational scenarios; verify data accuracy through cross-validation with sensor inputs; confirm availability via system uptime and interface responsiveness tests.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.5 s latency.

candidate_dps: DP1: Real-time telemetry ingestion and preprocessing pipeline.
DP2: Centralized worksite model database with consistency management.
DP3: Low-latency data distribution service using optimized network protocols.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1, DP2, and DP3 combined to address sub-FRs for timely acquisition, update, and distribution of worksite model data.

potential_sources: Data dependency between telemetry ingestion and model update.
Synchronization between model update and data distribution.
coupling_check.notes: The DPs are logically coupled but designed with clear interfaces and asynchronous processing to minimize tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Risk of data inconsistency if synchronization fails.
Risk of latency spikes under high load.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor and optimize network and processing pipelines continuously; consider fallback mechanisms for latency spikes.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for telemetry ingestion, worksite model update, and data distribution.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time telemetry data from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange between NGHE Solution and external modules.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated worksite model data to Vehicle and Teleoperation for operational use.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Communicates safety-related updates to Safety Services.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as the core system managing worksite model updates; low diffusion risk due to centralized processing.
Limited containment; mainly data source and consumer; moderate diffusion risk if duplicated processing occurs.
Partial containment for telemetry and tasking interfaces; moderate diffusion risk.
Minimal containment; mainly receives safety alerts; higher diffusion risk if safety logic is duplicated.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing worksite model update logic within NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports timeliness and safety requirements.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck in NGHE Solution if processing load spikes.
Dependency on network link reliability for timely updates.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Implement load balancing and redundancy within NGHE Solution; monitor network health and implement fallback strategies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are well matched by the selected DPs, with clear logical mapping and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk due to centralized containment in NGHE Solution.
Ensure no duplication of update logic across Vehicle or Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Containment scores support NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module, reducing diffusion.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed via latency measurements, data consistency checks, and interface monitoring within NGHE Solution.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibilities to NGHE Solution with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Add fallback and redundancy mechanisms in NGHE Solution to mitigate bottlenecks.
Define monitoring and alerting for network link health.

decision.justification: The current level of functional decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meets the acceptance criteria and architectural clarity for the worksite update latency requirement.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire and process telemetry data from vehicles and sensors within 0.5 s latency.
FR2: Update the centralized worksite model with processed data ensuring consistency and accuracy.
FR3: Distribute updated worksite model data to all relevant modules and stakeholders within 0.5 s latency.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
NGHE Solution has sufficient processing capacity.
open_risks: Potential latency spikes under high load.
Network link failures impacting update timeliness.
evidence_needed: Latency performance data under operational scenarios.
Load testing results for NGHE Solution processing.
